title: At the Dentist
summary: Olivia visits the dentist with a toothache and talks with Dr. Brian about her symptoms, a check-up, and her next appointment.

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: Good morning, Olivia. What seems to be the problem today?

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: Good morning, doctor. I've had a bad toothache for three days.

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: I see. Can you show me which tooth hurts?

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: It's this one at the back. It hurts when I drink something cold.

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: Let me take a look. Please open wide for me.

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: Is it serious? I'm a little worried.

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: Don't worry. You have a small cavity, but it's easy to fix.

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: That's a relief. Will it hurt during the treatment?

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: No, I'll give you an injection so you won't feel any pain.

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: Okay. How often should I come for a check-up?

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: You should have a check-up every six months.

body.speaker: Olivia
body.text: Then I'd like to make an appointment for next time.

body.speaker: Brian
body.text: Sure. The receptionist will help you book a date.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Good morning, Olivia.
sentences.translationVi: Chào buổi sáng, Olivia.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: What seems to be the problem today?
sentences.translationVi: Hôm nay chị có vấn đề gì vậy?

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: Good morning, doctor.
sentences.translationVi: Chào buổi sáng, bác sĩ.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: I've had a bad toothache for three days.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi bị đau răng nặng đã ba ngày rồi.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: I see.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hiểu rồi.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Can you show me which tooth hurts?
sentences.translationVi: Chị có thể chỉ cho tôi cái răng nào đau không?

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: It's this one at the back.
sentences.translationVi: Là cái này ở phía sau.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: It hurts when I drink something cold.
sentences.translationVi: Nó đau khi tôi uống đồ gì đó lạnh.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Let me take a look.
sentences.translationVi: Để tôi xem nào.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Please open wide for me.
sentences.translationVi: Chị hãy há to miệng giúp tôi nhé.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: Is it serious?
sentences.translationVi: Có nghiêm trọng không ạ?

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: I'm a little worried.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hơi lo lắng.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Don't worry.
sentences.translationVi: Đừng lo.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: You have a small cavity, but it's easy to fix.
sentences.translationVi: Chị có một lỗ sâu nhỏ, nhưng nó dễ chữa thôi.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: That's a relief.
sentences.translationVi: Thật nhẹ nhõm.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: Will it hurt during the treatment?
sentences.translationVi: Trong lúc điều trị có đau không ạ?

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: No, I'll give you an injection so you won't feel any pain.
sentences.translationVi: Không, tôi sẽ tiêm thuốc cho chị để chị không cảm thấy đau.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: Okay.
sentences.translationVi: Được rồi.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: How often should I come for a check-up?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi nên đến kiểm tra bao lâu một lần?

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: You should have a check-up every six months.
sentences.translationVi: Chị nên đi kiểm tra sáu tháng một lần.

sentences.speaker: Olivia
sentences.text: Then I'd like to make an appointment for next time.
sentences.translationVi: Vậy thì tôi muốn đặt lịch hẹn cho lần tới.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: Sure.
sentences.translationVi: Chắc chắn rồi.

sentences.speaker: Brian
sentences.text: The receptionist will help you book a date.
sentences.translationVi: Nhân viên lễ tân sẽ giúp chị đặt ngày.

annotations.phrase: What seems to be the problem
annotations.exampleEn: What seems to be the problem today?
I've had a bad toothache for three days.
Can you show me which tooth hurts?
It's this one at the back.
It hurts when I drink something cold.
You have a small cavity, but it's easy to fix.
You have a small cavity, but it's easy to fix.
Will it hurt during the treatment?
No, I'll give you an injection so you won't feel any pain.
You should have a check-up every six months.
Then I'd like to make an appointment for next time.
The receptionist will help you book a date.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for duration: 'I've had … for …'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại hoàn thành với 'for' để nói một tình trạng kéo dài bao lâu, bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn còn đến hiện tại. Ví dụ: 'I've had a bad toothache for three days.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect with 'for' to say how long a situation has lasted, starting in the past and continuing to now. Example: 'I've had a bad toothache for three days.'

grammarNotes.title: Giving advice with 'should'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'should + động từ nguyên mẫu' để đưa ra lời khuyên hoặc đề nghị. Ví dụ: 'You should have a check-up every six months.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'should + base verb' to give advice or a recommendation. Example: 'You should have a check-up every six months.'

Chị có thể chỉ cho tôi cái răng nào đau không? Olivia: Là cái này ở phía sau. Nó đau khi tôi uống đồ gì đó lạnh. Brian: Để tôi xem nào. Chị hãy há to miệng giúp tôi nhé. Olivia: Có nghiêm trọng không ạ? Tôi hơi lo lắng. Brian: Đừng lo. Chị có một lỗ sâu nhỏ, nhưng nó dễ chữa thôi. Olivia: Thật nhẹ nhõm. Trong lúc điều trị có đau không ạ? Brian: Không, tôi sẽ tiêm thuốc cho chị để chị không cảm thấy đau. Olivia: Được rồi. Tôi nên đến kiểm tra bao lâu một lần? Brian: Chị nên đi kiểm tra sáu tháng một lần. Olivia: Vậy thì tôi muốn đặt lịch hẹn cho lần tới. Brian: Chắc chắn rồi. Nhân viên lễ tân sẽ giúp chị đặt ngày.
criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think many people feel nervous about going to the dentist, and what could help them feel more comfortable?
